projectTitle: RestroNet: An AI-Powered Restaurant Discovery and Recommendation System
projectDescription: RestroNet is a full-stack, web-based restaurant discovery and reservation platform purpose-built for Kathmandu's dining ecosystem. It enables food enthusiasts to browse, search, and book curated restaurants using intelligent AI-driven recommendations, natural language search, and a conversational concierge chatbot. Restaurant owners can manage their menus, reservations, and operational data, while a superadmin panel provides complete platform oversight.
problemStatement: Diners in Kathmandu lack a centralized, intelligent platform to discover quality restaurants tailored to their individual tastes, mood, location, and budget. Existing local solutions are generic directory listings with no personalization, no real-time availability, and no mechanism for restaurant owners to digitally manage their operations. This creates friction on both sides: diners cannot efficiently find the right dining experience, and restaurant owners have no unified digital channel for visibility, reservations, and customer engagement. RestroNet solves this by combining a smart multi-factor recommendation engine, geospatial search, AI-generated insights, and a full reservation pipeline into a single platform.
objectives: Build a curated restaurant discovery platform focused on the Kathmandu dining scene with rich search and filter capabilities

Implement an AI-powered multi-factor recommendation engine that personalizes results using explicit preferences, implicit behavioral signals (reviews and reservations), location, mood, and time-of-day context
Develop a Content-Based Filtering (CBF) pipeline using TF-IDF vectors and Gemini text embeddings for semantic restaurant matching
Integrate natural language search via Gemini AI intent parsing and NLP-based rule parsing with Jaro-Winkler fuzzy matching
Provide a complete table reservation system with real-time capacity slot management and opening-hours validation
Build an AI Maitre D' conversational chatbot powered by Gemini 2.5 Flash that answers dining queries using live restaurant data
Implement digital menu management for restaurant owners including AI Vision OCR extraction from menu images
Automate diner engagement through scheduled email notifications: 24h and 2h reservation reminders, post-visit review requests, and personalized daily digest emails
Develop an AI-powered review aggregation pipeline that generates structured summaries and positives/constructives per restaurant
Build automated AI content moderation on review submission to filter spam and toxic content
Implement a trending detection algorithm to surface restaurants gaining momentum in review velocity
Create a comprehensive admin dashboard with real-time statistics, and full CRUD management of restaurants, owners, users, reservations, and reviews

backend.ai: Google Generative AI SDK (@google/generative-ai) — Gemini 2.5 Flash + text-embedding-004

modules.name: User Authentication & Profile Management
modules.description: JWT-based registration and login for two separate user pools: consumers (User model) and admins/owners (Admin model). Users store dietary preferences, meal type preferences, price range, and max distance for recommendation personalization. bcryptjs hashes passwords with 12 salt rounds. Profile update including avatar upload.

modules.name: Restaurant Discovery & Search
modules.description: Multi-tier search combining exact name matching, starts-with, contains, all-token, partial-token, Jaro-Winkler fuzzy match, cuisine match, category match, tag match, and description matching. Confidence-tier gating returns only the highest-confidence tier present. Produces spelling suggestions via Jaro-Winkler when zero results are found. Filterable by cuisine, category, tag, price range, mood, location radius, rating, and sort order.

modules.name: AI-Powered Recommendation Engine
modules.description: Multi-factor weighted scoring engine that scores all active venues against 11 signals: name text match, cuisine match, category match, tag match, price proximity, star rating, geospatial distance, mood tag overlap, review/reservation implicit behavioral profile, Gemini embedding cosine similarity, and log-normalized popularity. Weights are dynamically adjusted based on which signals are active. Applies diversity re-ranking to prevent cuisine monopoly in top results. Includes a time-of-day boost for meal-context-appropriate venues.

modules.name: Content-Based Filtering (CBF) Pipeline
modules.description: Pre-computes feature vectors for every restaurant: one-hot cuisine vector, one-hot tag vector, normalized price range (0–1), normalized rating (0–1), TF-IDF sparse vector from cuisine names + tag names + description, and a dense combined vector. Also generates 768-dimensional Gemini text-embedding-004 embeddings per venue. Stored in a dedicated RestaurantFeature collection. Can be batch-rebuilt by admin trigger.

modules.name: Natural Language Intent Parsing
modules.description: Two-layer NL parsing system. Primary: Gemini 2.5 Flash parses free-text queries into structured filters (cuisineIds, tagIds, categoryIds, priceRanges, mood, sortBy, isNearMe, isTopRated). Fallback: rule-based NLP parser using the natural library (Jaro-Winkler, tokenization) for offline/graceful-degradation mode. Mood-to-tag keyword mapping covers 8 moods: romantic, family-friendly, cafe, luxury, nightlife, casual, work-friendly, aesthetic.

modules.name: Table Reservation & Capacity Management
modules.description: Users create reservations with date, time slot, guest count, contact phone, and special requests. Capacity service computes available time slots for a given date by reading a venue's opening hours and slot duration, then subtracting already-booked confirmed/pending reservations from maxCapacity. Admin can update reservation status (pending → confirmed → completed / cancelled). Supports guest count of 1–20.

modules.name: Automated Email Notification System
modules.description: Four cron-scheduled automation jobs: (1) Reservation reminders at 24h and 2h before dining time — runs every hour. (2) Post-visit review request emails sent within 24h of a completed reservation (only if user has not already reviewed). (3) Trending venue detection runs daily at 02:00. (4) Personalized daily digest email at 08:00 to active users (logged in within 14 days, notifications enabled) with top 3 personalized venue picks.

modules.name: AI Maitre D' Conversational Concierge
modules.description: Floating chat widget on all public pages. Powered by Gemini 2.5 Flash with a system instruction that injects the live RestroNet restaurant directory (name, cuisines, tags, price, rating, location) into context. Maintains multi-turn conversation history. Responds in an elegant, brand-appropriate hospitality tone. Accessible without login.

modules.name: Digital Menu Management & AI OCR Extraction
modules.description: Admins/owners can create, edit, and delete menus per venue. Each menu has items with name, description, price (NPR), category, dietary flags (vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free), allergens, calories, and availability toggle. AI OCR endpoint uploads a menu image and calls Gemini Vision (multimodal) to extract structured dish data. AI also suggests 3 recommended dishes based on the menu and logged-in user preferences.

modules.name: AI Review Summarization
modules.description: When a venue's reviews exceed a snapshot threshold, Gemini 2.5 Flash generates a structured AI summary: a 2–3 sentence overall dining experience summary, up to 4 positives, and up to 2 constructive feedback points. Summary is cached in the Venue document (aiSummary field) with a reviewCountSnapshot to detect staleness. Displayed on the RestaurantDetail page.

modules.name: Automated AI Review Moderation
modules.description: On every review save, a Mongoose pre-save hook calls Gemini 2.5 Flash to classify the comment as clean, spam, or toxic. Constructive negative feedback is preserved; genuine violations trigger isApproved=false with a moderationNote. Fails open (approves) if the API is unavailable. Admins can also manually toggle review visibility from the admin panel.

modules.name: Trending Venue Detection
modules.description: Daily cron job computes a velocity score per venue: (recentWeekReviewCount - prevWeekReviewCount) / max(prevWeekReviewCount, 1). A venue is flagged isTrending=true if velocity > 0.5 AND recentCount >= 3. TrendingScore and isTrending flag are stored on the Venue document and surfaced as TrendingBadge components in the UI.

modules.name: Admin Dashboard & Platform Management
modules.description: Superadmin panel with a statistics dashboard (total venues, users, reservations, reviews), and full CRUD management for: Restaurants (with opening hours, capacity, gallery, menu), Owners (Admin role=owner accounts), Users (block/unblock, delete), Reservations (status updates), Reviews (visibility toggle). Includes OSM data import for seeding venue data from OpenStreetMap. Admin has a separate JWT auth flow.

modules.name: Geospatial Location Features
modules.description: Venues store GeoJSON Point coordinates (longitude, latitude) with a 2dsphere MongoDB index. User profiles store last known coordinates. Haversine formula calculates exact distance in km between user and each venue. Distance score uses exponential decay (score = e^(-d / 0.4*maxDist)). Used in search filters, map view on the Search page (Leaflet), and recommendation scoring.

modules.name: Dark/Light Theme System
modules.description: ThemeContext provides isDark state toggled globally. All color values use CSS custom properties (--background, --foreground, --primary, --border, --muted-foreground, etc.) defined in index.css with .dark class overrides. Supports the full dual-palette design system including dark-mode glassmorphic panel override (.rcard-panel) and admin panel dark utility surface.

algorithms.name: Multi-Factor Weighted Recommendation Scoring
algorithms.description: Scores every active venue against 11 weighted signals: name text match, cuisine overlap, category match, tag overlap, price proximity, star rating (0-1 normalized), geospatial distance, mood-tag overlap, implicit behavioral profile, Gemini embedding cosine similarity, and log-normalized popularity. Base weights are dynamically adjusted when specific signals are active (e.g., location adds 10% to distance weight). A featuredBoost (+8%) and timeBoost (+5%) are added post-weighting. A visitedPenalty (0.85x) softly demotes already-visited venues. Final scores are normalized to [0, 1].

algorithms.name: Content-Based Filtering (CBF) with TF-IDF and One-Hot Encoding
algorithms.description: Builds a feature vector per restaurant: one-hot cuisine vector (length = total cuisines), one-hot tag vector (length = total tags), normalized price (0.25–1.0), normalized rating (0–1), and a TF-IDF sparse map over combined text (cuisine names + tag names + description + venue name). These are concatenated into a dense combinedVector for cosine similarity computation.

algorithms.name: Cosine Similarity
algorithms.description: Computes dot product divided by the product of L2 norms of two numeric vectors. Used for: (a) comparing user preference vectors against venue feature vectors in CBF, and (b) comparing Gemini query embeddings against stored 768-dimensional venue embeddings for semantic search. Output normalized from [-1, 1] to [0, 1].

algorithms.name: Jaro-Winkler String Distance
algorithms.description: String similarity metric from the natural NLP library. Used in: (1) fuzzy venue name matching in the search cascade (threshold: 0.88 for match, 0.70 for suggestions), (2) NLP parser to match user query tokens against cuisine, tag, category, and mood keywords in the database with a threshold of 0.90.

algorithms.name: Haversine Distance Formula
algorithms.description: Calculates great-circle distance in kilometers between two (longitude, latitude) coordinate pairs using the spherical law of cosines. Used to compute user-to-venue distances for distance-aware recommendation scoring and location-based filtering with a configurable maxDistanceKm radius (default: 20 km).

algorithms.name: Implicit Behavioral Profile Builder
algorithms.description: Derives user cuisine, tag, and price affinity scores from historical behavior without explicit input. Positive reviews (4–5 stars) carry weights of 1.0 (5★) and 0.7 (4★). Completed/confirmed reservations carry 0.4 weight. Frequencies are normalized so the top item = 1.0. Profiles are built on-demand per recommendation request from the last 30 reviews and 20 reservations.

algorithms.name: Diversity Re-ranking
algorithms.description: After score-sorted ranking, iterates the candidate list and caps any single primary cuisine from occupying more than 40% of the top-N results (ceil(N * 0.40) slots). Overflow items are deferred and used to fill remaining slots after the primary pass. Prevents a single dominant cuisine from monopolizing the recommendation feed.

algorithms.name: Review Velocity Trending Score
algorithms.description: Computes momentum for each venue by comparing review counts in the most recent 7-day window vs the prior 7-day window: velocityScore = (recentCount - prevCount) / max(prevCount, 1). A venue is marked trending if velocityScore > 0.5 AND recentCount >= 3. Runs as a daily cron job at 02:00.

algorithms.name: Time-of-Day Meal Context Boost
algorithms.description: At scoring time, checks the current server hour and maps it to a meal context (breakfast: 06–11, lunch: 11–15, snacks: 15–18, dinner: 18–23). If the venue's mealTypes array includes the current context, the venue receives a +5% boost on its final score.

algorithms.name: Tiered Text Match Cascade with Confidence Gating
algorithms.description: 10-tier descending cascade for text search: (1) exact name match → 1.0, (2) name starts-with → 0.95, (3) name contains → 0.90, (4) all tokens in name → 0.85, (5) partial tokens → ratio*0.65, (6) fuzzy Jaro-Winkler name → sim*0.5, (7) cuisine match → 0.50, (8) category match → 0.40, (9) tag match → 0.30, (10) description match → 0.25/0.15. Confidence gating then returns only the highest-confidence tier present, preventing weak matches from appearing alongside strong ones.

algorithms.name: Log-Normalized Popularity Scoring with Cold-Start Boost
algorithms.description: Popularity score = log(1 + reviewCount) / log(1 + maxReviews), normalizing review count on a logarithmic scale so popular venues score higher without dominating. Venues created within the last 30 days receive a guaranteed baseline score of 0.35 regardless of review count, preventing cold-start penalization of newly added restaurants.

algorithms.name: Exponential Decay Distance Scoring
algorithms.description: Converts raw distance in km to a [0, 1] score using exponential decay: score = e^(-distance / (maxDistance * 0.4)). Score = 1.0 at 0 km and approximately 0.37 at half the max distance. This rewards nearby venues strongly while allowing distant venues to still appear if other signals are strong.

auth: PUT    /api/auth/profile           — Update user profile and preferences (protected)
venues: GET    /api/venues                 — List/search all venues (public, with optional admin context)
GET    /api/venues/:idOrSlug       — Get single venue by ID or slug (public)
GET    /api/venues/:id/menu-suggestions — AI menu dish suggestions (optional auth)
GET    /api/venues/:id/slots       — Get available booking slots for a date (public)
GET    /api/venues/search-external — Search OpenStreetMap for venues (admin only)
POST   /api/venues/import-osm      — Import venue from OSM data (admin only)
reviews: GET    /api/reviews/venue/:venueId — Get paginated reviews for a venue (public)
GET    /api/reviews/admin          — Get all reviews for admin (admin only)
reservations: GET    /api/reservations/my        — Get current user's reservations (user protected)
menus: GET    /api/menus/venue/:venueId   — Get menus for a venue (public)
POST   /api/menus/venue/:venueId   — Create menu for venue (admin only)
POST   /api/menus/venue/:venueId/extract-ocr — AI OCR extract menu from image (admin only)
recommendations: POST   /api/recommendations/admin/rebuild-vectors   — Rebuild all CBF feature vectors (admin)
POST   /api/recommendations/admin/rebuild-summaries — Rebuild all AI review summaries (admin)
chat: POST   /api/chat                   — Send message to AI Maitre D' concierge (public)

databaseModels.description: Consumer accounts. Stores name, email, hashed password, avatar, phone, preferences (cuisines, tags, priceRange, mealTypes, maxDistanceKm), last known GeoJSON location, emailNotifications flag, lastLoginAt. Includes a hasPreferences virtual and comparePassword instance method.
Platform admin and restaurant owner accounts. Role enum: superadmin | owner. Stores name, email, hashed password, isActive, lastLogin. Owners are linked to their Venue via the Venue.owner field.

Core entity. Stores name, slug (auto-generated), description, address (street/city/state/country/postalCode), GeoJSON location coordinates, cuisines/tags/category refs, priceRange (1-4), day-by-day openingHours, mealTypes, contact info, logo, gallery images, menu images, denormalized averageRating and totalReviews, featureVector ref, aiSummary (summaryText, positives, constructives, reviewCountSnapshot, lastUpdated), 768-dim Gemini embedding, owner ref, isActive, isFeatured, isTrending, trendingScore, maxCapacity, slotDurationMinutes, and OSM source fields.

One review per user per venue enforced by compound unique index. Granular rating breakdown: overall (required), food, service, ambience, value (all 1-5). Includes title, comment, photo URLs, isApproved (AI moderated on pre-save), isHidden (admin toggle), moderationNote, and helpfulVotes array. Static recalculateRating aggregates average and count back to Venue on post-save and post-delete hooks.

Links a User to a Venue with date, time slot string (HH:MM), guest count, status (pending|confirmed|cancelled|completed), specialRequests, contactPhone. Tracks reminder and review request sent flags: reminderSent24h, reminderSent2h, reviewRequestSent.

A venue can have multiple named menus (e.g. Lunch Menu, Dinner Menu). Each menu embeds an array of MenuItems with: name, description, price (NPR), category, image, isVegetarian, isVegan, isGlutenFree, isAvailable, isFeatured, allergens, calories.

CBF vector store. One document per venue. Stores: cuisineVector (one-hot array), tagVector (one-hot array), normalizedPriceRange, normalizedRating, tfidfVector (Map<term, tfidf>), combinedVector (concatenated dense vector), lastComputedAt, version.
Metadata entity. Name, slug (auto-generated), description, icon, isActive.
Metadata entity for restaurant attributes (e.g. 'Rooftop', 'Live Music', 'Pet Friendly'). Name, slug, description, category enum (ambience|service|food|facility|occasion|other), icon, isActive.
Metadata entity for venue categories (e.g. 'Fine Dining', 'Cafe', 'Fast Food', 'Bakery'). Name, slug, description, icon, isActive.

frontendPages: Home — Hero, featured restaurants carousel, category grid (Fine Dining, Cafe, Fast Food, Pub), AI-powered picks, marquee strip, stats
Search — Split-pane: scrollable venue list (left) + Leaflet interactive map (right), pill chip filters for cuisine/price/mood, distance slider
Discover — AI natural language search with Gemini intent parsing, mood-based discovery cards, filter-based recommendations

RestaurantDetail — Full venue page: gallery swiper, AI review summary, menu display with AI dish suggestions, multi-dimensional rating breakdown, reviews list, capacity-aware reservation booking widget
Register — User signup with preference selection
Profile — Edit name, avatar, phone, dietary preferences, max distance
MyReservations — List of all user reservations with status badges and cancellation
AdminDashboard — Stats cards (venues, users, reservations, reviews), Recharts graphs
AdminRestaurants — Restaurant CRUD with opening hours management, gallery upload, AI OCR menu import
AdminUsers — User list with block/unblock and delete
AdminReservations — All reservations with status management
AdminReviews — All reviews with visibility toggle

methodology.rationale: The project was built in clearly defined feature phases evidenced by the docs/superpowers/plans/ directory: Phase 1 (Automation Backbone — reservation reminders, trending, digest), Phase 2 (Personalization Depth — CBF pipeline, embeddings, implicit profiling), Phase 3 (Owner and Admin Intelligence — menu OCR, review summaries, capacity management). Each phase delivered working, tested increments. Test files (Jest) exist for unit-testing core algorithms (capacityService, trendingService, openingHoursValidator) separately from integration concerns.

included: Web-based platform (desktop and mobile responsive) for the Kathmandu restaurant ecosystem
Three distinct user roles: Diner (consumer), Restaurant Owner, Superadmin
Complete restaurant profile management including opening hours, location, gallery, digital menus
AI-powered restaurant recommendations personalized to each logged-in diner
Natural language restaurant search powered by Gemini AI and NLP fallback
Table reservation with real-time slot availability based on capacity and opening hours
Structured digital menu management with AI Vision OCR from scanned/photographed menus
AI Maitre D' chatbot available on all pages for conversational dining guidance
Automated email engagement: pre-reservation reminders, post-visit review requests, daily personalized digest
AI-generated review summaries and automated content moderation on review submission
Trending venue detection based on review velocity
Geospatial distance-aware search and recommendation using device location
Full admin panel for platform governance (restaurant, user, review, reservation management)
Dark and light UI theme with full design system consistency
excluded: Real-time payment processing or online payment gateway

limitations: No real-time updates — reservation status changes and new reviews require a page reload; WebSockets (e.g. Socket.io) are not implemented
No payment gateway integration — reservations are booking-only with no deposit or online payment
Single-city scope — the platform is built specifically for Kathmandu with no multi-city architecture
Gemini API dependency — AI features (recommendations, chat, OCR, review summaries) fail gracefully but degrade if the API key is missing or rate-limited
Email delivery via Nodemailer without a transactional email service (no SendGrid/Mailgun) — deliverability depends on the SMTP provider configured in .env
No image CDN — uploaded images (gallery, menus, avatars) are stored on the local filesystem in /uploads, making horizontal scaling without shared storage difficult
Rate limiting is disabled in the current server configuration (limiter middleware is commented out in server.js)
No collaborative filtering — recommendations are content-based only; user-to-user similarity (what similar diners liked) is not implemented
Concierge chat history is managed in frontend React state only — history is lost on page refresh
OSM venue import is a manual admin action; there is no automated periodic sync with OpenStreetMap data
CBF feature vector rebuild must be manually triggered by an admin after adding new cuisine or tag metadata
No email verification on user registration — accounts are activated immediately without confirming the email address
Review photos can be attached but there is no image moderation on photo uploads
futureRecommendations: Develop a Progressive Web App (PWA) or native mobile application (React Native) to reach mobile-first users in Kathmandu
Integrate a local payment gateway (eSewa or Khalti) to support reservation deposits and pre-payment, reducing no-shows
Implement WebSocket (Socket.io) for real-time reservation status notifications and live availability updates
Add Collaborative Filtering (CF) to complement the existing CBF engine — a hybrid recommender using user-to-user similarity would improve cold-start performance
Expand to a multi-city architecture to cover other Nepali cities (Pokhara, Bhaktapur) and eventually other South Asian markets
Introduce a restaurant loyalty and rewards system (points per reservation, redeemable discounts) to increase retention
Move uploaded media to a CDN-backed object storage (AWS S3 / Cloudflare R2) to support horizontal scaling and faster global delivery
Integrate a transactional email service (SendGrid, Mailgun, or Amazon SES) in place of raw Nodemailer SMTP for reliable deliverability and delivery analytics
Implement email address verification on registration to reduce fake accounts
Add Nepali language support (i18n) to make the platform accessible to non-English-speaking restaurant owners and diners
Build an analytics dashboard for restaurant owners showing reservation trends, peak hours, popular menu items, and review sentiment over time
Implement automated periodic OSM data sync to keep restaurant data fresh without manual admin intervention
Add photo moderation for review photos using Gemini Vision to detect inappropriate content
Implement push notifications via Web Push API to replace/complement email reminders
Add a table layout / floor plan manager for restaurant owners to assign specific tables to reservations
